title: Critical editor fixes: undo/redo, arrow redesign, annotation-shadow bug
goal: Fix the things that make the current editor feel unpolished. These are the worst bugs from the user's report; each task starts by REPRODUCING the issue (per CLAUDE.md) before fixing. TDD: arrow geometry + shadow extent are PhotonzCore/PhotonzRender work — tests first.

tasks.title: Undo/redo wired across the whole editor (⌘Z / ⇧⌘Z)
tasks.notes: ROOT CAUSE (reproduced via in-process NSApp.mainMenu dump): the Edit-menu commands used `CommandGroup(after: .undoRedo)`, which does NOT remove SwiftUI's built-in Undo/Redo items. The built-ins kept the ⌘Z/⇧⌘Z key equivalents and target the responder-chain `undo:`/`redo:` UndoManager (which we never register operations with → dead), while our own Undo/Redo buttons ended up with NO shortcut. So ⌘Z went to a no-op. FIX: one-line change to `CommandGroup(replacing: .undoRedo)` in PhotonzApp.swift (+comment). Re-dumped menu: now exactly one Undo[⌘Z]/Redo[⇧⌘Z] pair bound to our commands; instrumented the commands body and confirmed SwiftUI re-evaluates & observes appState.canUndo/canRedo (false→true after an edit). NO mutation paths bypass History — every editor mutation already routes through AppState.perform → history.perform (verified by grep; preview drags intentionally mutate a local copy and commit one undo step on mouse-up). So no new core tests were needed. NOTE: synthetic NSApp.sendEvent/performKeyEquivalent/CGEvent.postToPid do NOT drive SwiftUI's command key-dispatch in-process (the shortcut is caught by a SwiftUI event monitor that only fires for real window-server events), so the final ⌘Z keypress was not machine-driven — but the user physically pressed ⌘Z/⇧⌘Z and CONFIRMED it works (2026-06-17). 367 tests green.

tasks.title: Arrow redesign: bold head + user-adjustable size + per-object inspector
tasks.notes: SHIPPED (2026-06-18): Geometry.arrowhead redesigned bolder with a `scale` param; Geometry.arrowShaftEnd added so the shaft stops INSIDE the head (round cap no longer pokes past the sharp tip) — used by both AnnotationRasterizer and the CanvasView live preview. New `arrowheadScale` on AnnotationContent + AnnotationStyles (backward-compat custom Codable, decodeIfPresent). Threaded through rasterizer + renderPadding. UI: toolbar style popover now uses SLIDERS (Width 1-40pt, Arrowhead ×0.5-×5) replacing the old discrete dots; AppState.previewAnnotationRestyle previews live and setAnnotationStrokeWidth/setAnnotationArrowheadScale commit one undo step. Also added a per-object AnnotationInspector in the Layers panel (color/thickness/head-size). 374 tests green (GeometryTests + an AnnotationRendering scale regression test). REMAINING FOLLOW-UPS from user feedback → see 10.4. DEFERRED to phase 14: curved-arrow variant + tail flair + arrow style set (per competitive-cleanshot.md).

tasks.title: Annotation shadow follows the stroke (render) + adjustable shadow exposed
tasks.notes: RENDER WAS ALREADY CORRECT (reproduced 2026-06-17): DocumentRenderer derives the shadow from the rasterized annotation's ALPHA silhouette (CIColorMatrix, DocumentRenderer.swift:~432), so a diagonal arrow's shadow hugs the stroke, NOT its bbox — verified by pixel test (off-diagonal bbox corners stay white) + visual (red arrow on red bg visible via its hugging shadow). The 'boxed shadow' report did not reproduce. Adjustable shadow is now exposed in the Layers-panel LayerInspector (blur/size/direction/opacity/color). NOTE: a NEW shadow bug surfaced in user testing ('when I select shadow, nothing appears') — tracked separately in 10.6.

tasks.title: Arrow polish round 2: 1.0 default + decouple head size from thickness
tasks.notes: SHIPPED (2026-06-18). (1) DEFAULT: AnnotationStyles.defaultArrowheadScale 1.5 → 1.0. (2) DECOUPLE: Geometry now sizes the head from `scale` ALONE via fixed bases (baseArrowheadHalfWidth=16, baseArrowheadLength=30, both private), so the Thickness slider no longer bloats the head. Chose 16/30 because ×1.0 of them ≈ the OLD strokeWidth-driven head at a 4px shaft/×1.5 — default arrows look unchanged. KEPT the thick-shaft floor: arrowheadHalfWidth = max(16*scale, strokeWidth*0.6) and rawArrowheadLength = max(30*scale, strokeWidth*1.1), so a very heavy line can never out-width its own head. Short-arrow length cap (min 85% of length) + arrowShaftEnd untouched. arrowheadHalfWidth stays the single source feeding AnnotationContent.renderPadding (Tools.swift) → frame padding in lockstep automatically. TESTS (TDD, red→green): replaced GeometryTests.arrowheadScalesWithStrokeWidth with arrowheadSizeIsIndependentOfStrokeWidth (same head for sw 2 vs 10 @ scale 1); added arrowheadFloorKeepsAVeryThickShaftFromOutWidthingItsHead (sw 60 head ≥ 60 wide); rewrote arrowheadIsClearlyWiderThanTheShaft → arrowheadIsAClearlyVisibleHead (absolute ≥24 bar + ≥ shaft); added AnnotationEditingTests.defaultArrowheadScaleIsOne; updated strokeWidthChangeRepadsTheFrameAroundFixedEndpoints to use sw 4→30 (only at heavy strokes does the floor move arrow padding now). 376 tests green; app builds.

tasks.title: Inspector → docked full-height resizable side panel with reorderable sections
tasks.notes: USER FEEDBACK 2026-06-18: 'the whole layer overlay should be a side panel, top to bottom, with a 1px sizer border on the left edge. Within it, sections that can be rearranged by dragging up/down, like Photoshop. But elegant and modern.' TODAY: LayersPanel is a FLOATING glass overlay (fixed width 248, maxHeight ~560) positioned in EditorView's ZStack. CHANGE TO: a docked RIGHT side panel spanning the full window height (top to bottom), with a 1px draggable resize handle on its LEFT edge (drag to set width; persist width to UserDefaults; sensible min/max). Restructure EditorView from ZStack-overlay to an HStack(canvas | resizable panel). INSIDE the panel: collapsible SECTIONS (Layers list, Annotation properties, Effects, Shadow, …) that the user can REORDER by dragging a section header up/down (Photoshop-style, but elegant/modern — clean headers, smooth expand/collapse, subtle drag affordance). Persist section order + collapsed state. Keep ⌘⌥L to toggle the whole panel. This is the biggest item; consider a small SectionStack component holding an ordered, draggable list of titled collapsible sections.

tasks.title: Shadow: fix invisible controls + add a SIZE (spread) knob distinct from Distance
tasks.notes: USER FEEDBACK 2026-06-18: 'when I select shadow, nothing appears', then 'shadow is missing a distance slider', then 'we NEED shadow size too'. TWO parts: (A) BUG — the existing shadow controls don't show. LayerInspector.shadowControls (LayersPanel.swift) has a Toggle then sliders shown only `if let shadow = style.shadow` (Blur/Color/Distance[offset magnitude]/Direction[offset angle]/Opacity). User not seeing them is almost certainly clipping: the panel maxHeight + the new AnnotationInspector pushed content into a ScrollView that may not expand — most likely fixed by 10.5's full-height panel. Also verify enabling Shadow renders a visibly default shadow. REPRODUCE FIRST. (B) NEW FEATURE — add a SHADOW SIZE (spread) control, SEPARATE from Distance (offset) and Blur (softness). Size = how much bigger/smaller the shadow SHAPE is than the object. Add `spread: CGFloat` (default 0) to PhotonzCore.ShadowStyle (backward-compat custom Codable decodeIfPresent ?? 0). Renderer (DocumentRenderer shadow step, ~line 432): before the gaussian blur, dilate the alpha silhouette by `spread` via CIMorphologyMaximum (radius=spread); negative spread → CIMorphologyMinimum (erode). TDD a pixel test: positive spread makes the shadow extend further from the stroke than spread 0. UI: add a 'Shadow Size' slider (range e.g. 0...20, or -10...20) next to Blur/Distance/Direction. FINAL shadow knob set: Blur (softness), Size (spread), Distance (offset), Direction (angle), Color, Opacity.

tasks.title: Perf: layer selection is slow — must be instant
tasks.notes: USER FEEDBACK 2026-06-18: 'when I change layers, it's very slow to select, should be instant.' REPRODUCE + PROFILE. selectLayer(_:) should be a pure state flip (set selectedLayerID) with no re-render of the document. Suspects: (a) selection triggers thumbnail regeneration (AppState.thumbnail(for:)) for layers — check it's cached and not recomputed on every selection/inspector show; (b) selection causes a full composite re-render or submit; (c) the inspector/AnnotationInspector recomputes heavy work (previewedStyle, document lookups) on each selection; (d) SwiftUI re-evaluating the whole panel list. Make selection O(1) state-only. Add a perf note in the commit per CLAUDE.md if the composite path is touched.

tasks.title: Bug: double-click on window background doesn't zoom/maximize
tasks.notes: USER FEEDBACK 2026-06-18: 'when I double click on the background edges behind the image, the window does not maximize.' Standard macOS: double-clicking the titlebar/background performs the system 'double-click title bar to' action (Zoom/Minimize). The app uses .windowStyle(.hiddenTitleBar) (PhotonzApp.swift), which removes the titlebar so the draggable background no longer handles double-click-to-zoom. FIX: add a double-click handler on the window background / draggable chrome region that calls NSWindow.zoom(nil) (respect the user's 'Double-click a window's title bar to' setting via NSWorkspace/AppleActionOnDoubleClick if feasible, else default to zoom). Likely an NSView (or .onTapGesture(count:2) on the background) wired to the hosting NSWindow. Verify it doesn't intercept double-clicks meant for the canvas (only the empty background/edges behind the image).
